goal: Replace O(n^2) pairwise coupling loop with inverted-index candidate pruning to reduce complexity to O(E + C)

tasks.name: Replace O(n^2) loop with inverted-index candidate generation
microSteps: Run existing test suite to confirm GREEN baseline
Add _build_candidate_pairs() function that builds neighbor-to-symbols inverted index and returns set of frozenset candidate pairs
Replace the nested for-i/for-j loop in compute_coupling with iteration over candidate pairs from _build_candidate_pairs
Preserve canonical ordering (sorted IDs) and all existing behavior
Run existing test suite to confirm GREEN after refactor

tdd.reason: Pure refactor — green-refactor-green cycle. Existing 14 tests serve as regression gate.

tasks.action: Add a _build_candidate_pairs(neighbor_sets) function that: (1) builds an inverted index mapping each neighbor to the set of symbol node_ids that have it as a neighbor, (2) iterates over posting lists to collect candidate pairs (as frozensets to deduplicate), (3) returns a set of (a_id, b_id) tuples with canonical ordering. Then replace the O(n^2) nested loop in compute_coupling (lines 91-142) with iteration over these candidate pairs. Keep the Jaccard computation, threshold check, CouplingResult creation, and COUPLED_WITH relationship creation identical.

tasks.name: Add scaling regression test for inverted-index correctness
microSteps: Write a test that creates 50 symbol nodes with sparse shared neighbors (only a few pairs sharing neighbors)
Assert that compute_coupling returns only the expected coupled pairs and no false positives
Run the new test to confirm GREEN
tasks.action: Add test_sparse_large_graph_coupling to test_context_coupling.py: create 50 function nodes, wire only 2 pairs to share neighbors (pair A-B shares s1+s2, pair C-D shares s3), leave remaining 46 nodes isolated or with unique neighbors. Assert compute_coupling returns exactly 2 results with correct strengths. This validates the inverted-index doesn't miss or invent pairs at scale.

planVerify: python3 -c "from scripts.context.phases.coupling import compute_coupling, CouplingResult, _build_candidate_pairs; print('imports OK')"
commitMessage: perf(coupling): replace O(n^2) loop with inverted-index candidate pruning
